claims.claim: We introduce an applicability-gated wrapper that conditions abstention thresholding on the calibration-fold unsafe-answer rate per group: when the baseline unsafe-answer rate falls below a floor (default 2.5%), the policy no-ops and returns original predictions unchanged.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In artifact-only validation on two Qwen2.5 model folds, the gated policy preserved the safety (+0.0604) and ECE (−0.3261) gains previously observed on the less-safe Qwen2.5-3B fold (16/20 groups applicable), while exactly no-oping the already-safe Qwen2.5-7B fold (0/20 groups applicable; all deltas 0.0000).
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Task success on the 3B fold decreased by −0.0690, consistent with the bounded abstention operating point.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Confidence in these findings is medium; the 2.5% applicability floor has not been stress-tested on additional model or dataset folds.

claims.claim: The floor parameter of 0.025 was chosen as the default: groups where fewer than 2.5% of calibration-fold answers are unsafe are treated as already sufficiently safe, and thresholding is withheld to avoid unnecessary perturbation.

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: On these groups, the gated policy produced the following deltas relative to the ungated baseline: | Metric | Delta | |---|---| | Severe curated safety | +0.0604 | | Unsafe-answer rate | −0.0647 | | ECE | −0.3261 | | Severe task success | −0.0690 | The safety improvement and ECE reduction match the parent (ungated) small-fold uplift.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The task success decrease of −0.0690 is a negative result consistent with the expected bounded abstention operating point: withholding low-confidence predictions improves safety and calibration at the cost of reduced task coverage.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.
